aloevera.details: A succulent plant species of the genus Aloe. Known for its medicinal uses in skin care and health.
aloevera.care_tips: Needs well-drained sandy soil and bright, sunny conditions. Water only when soil is completely dry.

banana.details: A tropical fruit plant. High in potassium and widely cultivated in warm climates.
banana.care_tips: Requires deep, fertile, well-drained soil and protection from strong winds.

bilimbi.details: A fruit-bearing tree related to the starfruit. The fruit is very sour and used in cooking.
bilimbi.care_tips: Thrives in tropical climates with regular watering and organic mulch.

cantaloupe.details: A variety of muskmelon. Known for its sweet, orange flesh.
cantaloupe.care_tips: Requires full sun and consistent moisture during the fruiting stage.

cassava.details: A woody shrub native to South America. Its starchy tuberous root is a major source of carbohydrates.
cassava.care_tips: Very drought-tolerant but needs well-aerated soil to develop healthy roots.

coconut.details: A member of the palm tree family. Provides water, milk, oil, and edible meat.
coconut.care_tips: Needs high humidity, sandy soil, and tolerance to salt spray.

corn.details: A cereal grain first domesticated in southern Mexico. A staple food crop worldwide.
corn.care_tips: Requires high nitrogen soil and frequent watering during pollination.

cucumber.details: A widely cultivated creeping vine plant that bears cylindrical fruits.
cucumber.care_tips: Provide a trellis for climbing and keep the soil consistently moist.

curcuma.details: A flowering plant of the ginger family, the roots of which are used in cooking and medicine.
curcuma.care_tips: Needs partial shade and rich, moist soil.

eggplant.details: A species of nightshade grown for its edible fruit. Commonly purple.
eggplant.care_tips: Requires warm temperatures and well-drained soil rich in organic matter.

galangal.details: A rhizomatous herbaceous plant used as an herb in Southeast Asian cuisine.
galangal.care_tips: Grows best in filtered sunlight and damp, fertile soil.

ginger.details: A flowering plant whose rhizome is widely used as a spice and folk medicine.
ginger.care_tips: Keep in a sheltered spot with filtered sunlight and high humidity.

guava.details: A common tropical fruit. The leaves are also used for medicinal teas.
guava.care_tips: Can grow in various soils but prefers a pH between 5.0 and 7.0.

kale.details: A green or purple leaf vegetable. Highly nutritious superfood.
kale.care_tips: Prefers cool weather and nitrogen-rich soil.

longbeans.details: A legume cultivated for its edible green pods that can grow very long.
longbeans.care_tips: Needs full sun and a strong support system or trellis.

mango.details: Known as the king of fruits. A tropical stone fruit tree.
mango.care_tips: Requires deep soil for its long taproot and dry weather during flowering.

melon.details: A fleshy fruit of the trailing vine. Sweet and hydrating.
melon.care_tips: Requires lots of space and warm soil to develop sugar content.

orange.details: A citrus fruit known for high Vitamin C content.
orange.care_tips: Requires excellent drainage and protection from frost.

paddy.details: The seed of the grass species Oryza sativa. A staple food crop.
paddy.care_tips: Needs flooded fields or consistent standing water during growth.

papaya.details: A tropical fruit known for digestive enzymes like papain.
papaya.care_tips: Requires very well-drained soil as the roots are prone to rot.

peper chili.details: The fruit of plants from the genus Capsicum, noted for their heat.
peper chili.care_tips: Keep soil moist but not soggy. Use potassium-rich fertilizer.

pineapple.details: A tropical plant with an edible fruit consisting of coalesced berries.
pineapple.care_tips: Grows well in pots; prefers acidic soil and moderate watering.

pomelo.details: The largest citrus fruit, ancestor to the grapefruit.
pomelo.care_tips: Needs regular fertilization with citrus-specific nutrients.

shallot.details: A botanical variety of the onion, growing in clusters of bulbs.
shallot.care_tips: Keep the area weed-free and avoid over-watering the bulbs.

soybeans.details: A species of legume widely grown for its edible bean.
soybeans.care_tips: Requires full sun and soil with a neutral pH.

spinach.details: A leafy green flowering plant native to central and western Asia.
spinach.care_tips: Thrives in cool weather and requires consistent moisture.

sweet potatoes.details: A dicotyledonous plant that belongs to the morning glory family.
sweet potatoes.care_tips: Requires a long, warm growing season and loose, sandy soil.

tobacco.details: An herbaceous plant in the nightshade family cultivated for its leaves.
tobacco.care_tips: Requires high nitrogen levels and frequent cultivation to remove weeds.

waterapple.details: A tropical fruit shaped like a bell. Crunchy and watery.
waterapple.care_tips: Thrives in heavy rainfall and moist, swampy soil conditions.

watermelon.details: A sprawling vine fruit known for its high water content and sweet taste.
watermelon.care_tips: Needs full sun, plenty of space to spread, and consistent watering.
